forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8280'
+project: plugin-tsonly
+title: 'SDK: name and version as Plugin class props'
+---
+## What
+
+Move a plugin's `name` and `version` out of `plugin.json` and into the `Plugin` base class as plugin-author-set TypeScript properties.
+
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts` ŌĆö add `name` and `version` to the `Plugin` abstract base class. They are author-facing metadata a subclass sets (overridable `readonly` fields with sensible defaults, e.g. `name = "unnamed plugin"`, `version = "0.0.0"`, which a subclass overrides). Document in the class doc comment that they are **descriptive metadata only** ŌĆö the platform does not use them for identity or discovery (a plugin's identity is its bundle directory name) and the SDK never sends them to the host. Keep the existing `Plugin` surface (`load`/`unload`/`register`/`unregister`/`track`/`log`) intact.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/codegen.rs` ŌĆö if `TypesEmitter` / the generated `app.d.ts` describes the `Plugin` class surface, regenerate it so `name`/`version` appear. If codegen does not touch the `Plugin` class, no change ŌĆö confirm and note it.
+
+This task is host-Rust-free and SDK-only; it can land independently of the discovery work.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `Plugin` exposes `name` and `version`; a subclass can set them; defaults exist so a subclass that omits them still compiles.
+- [ ] The class doc comment states `name`/`version` are metadata only and are not used for identity/discovery.
+- [ ] `codegen.rs` / `app.d.ts` reflects the new props, or it is confirmed codegen does not cover the `Plugin` class.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Extend `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/sdk.rs` (the SDK transpile/dispatch test): a `Plugin` subclass that sets `name`/`version` transpiles cleanly and the properties are readable on the constructed instance.
+- [ ] If `codegen.rs` has a test asserting `app.d.ts` content, update it to expect `name`/`version`.
+- [ ] Run `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --test sdk` and any `codegen` test ŌĆö pass.
+- [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --all-targets -- -D warnings` ŌĆö clean.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the failing SDK test first, then update `plugin.ts`.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -16,15 +16,19 @@
 This task is host-Rust-free and SDK-only; it can land independently of the discovery work.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `Plugin` exposes `name` and `version`; a subclass can set them; defaults exist so a subclass that omits them still compiles.
-- [ ] The class doc comment states `name`/`version` are metadata only and are not used for identity/discovery.
-- [ ] `codegen.rs` / `app.d.ts` reflects the new props, or it is confirmed codegen does not cover the `Plugin` class.
+- [x] `Plugin` exposes `name` and `version`; a subclass can set them; defaults exist so a subclass that omits them still compiles.
+- [x] The class doc comment states `name`/`version` are metadata only and are not used for identity/discovery.
+- [x] `codegen.rs` / `app.d.ts` reflects the new props, or it is confirmed codegen does not cover the `Plugin` class.
+
+## Codegen note
+
+Confirmed: `codegen.rs` does NOT cover the SDK `Plugin` class surface, so no codegen change was needed. `render_declaration` emits a `declare global { interface Plugin { readonly [K in keyof App]: App[K]; } }` block ŌĆö a TypeScript *interface* named `Plugin` that types the dynamic per-server dispatcher index (`this.<server>...`). It is a distinct declaration from the SDK's `Plugin` *abstract class* in `plugin.ts`; codegen never describes the class's static members (`load`/`unload`/`register`/`name`/`version`), only the dynamic server proxy. The `name`/`version` fields therefore live entirely in `plugin.ts` and the codegen output and its tests are unaffected.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Extend `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/sdk.rs` (the SDK transpile/dispatch test): a `Plugin` subclass that sets `name`/`version` transpiles cleanly and the properties are readable on the constructed instance.
-- [ ] If `codegen.rs` has a test asserting `app.d.ts` content, update it to expect `name`/`version`.
-- [ ] Run `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --test sdk` and any `codegen` test ŌĆö pass.
-- [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --all-targets -- -D warnings` ŌĆö clean.
+- [x] Extend `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/sdk.rs` (the SDK transpile/dispatch test): a `Plugin` subclass that sets `name`/`version` transpiles cleanly and the properties are readable on the constructed instance.
+- [x] If `codegen.rs` has a test asserting `app.d.ts` content, update it to expect `name`/`version`. ŌĆö N/A: codegen does not cover the `Plugin` class (see note above); codegen tests unchanged.
+- [x] Run `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --test sdk` and any `codegen` test ŌĆö pass. (7 sdk + 6 codegen tests pass)
+- [x] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --all-targets -- -D warnings` ŌĆö clean.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the failing SDK test first, then update `plugin.ts`.
\ No newline at end of file
